demographics.value: Female (gender inferred from clinical notes using 'she'). Full name, age not documented in legible text.

secondary_diagnoses.value: 1) Urinary Tract Infection; 2) Known case of Thyroid disorder on treatment; 3) Grade I fatty liver (incidental finding on USG); 4) Cholelithiasis without cholecystitis (incidental finding on USG)

hospital_course.value: Patient presented with multiple episodes of loose stools, 2-3 episodes of vomiting, fatigue for 3 days, and fever since previous day. Initially treated at local clinic before presenting to this facility. On admission: PR-89/min, BP-130/80 mmHg, RR-20/min, SPO2-98% on room air, conscious and oriented. Initial investigations showed normal CBC, elevated serum creatinine (1.65mg/dl), low serum sodium (125.00mmol/L). Urine routine showed ketone bodies (+), 10-12/hpf pus cells, 13-20/hpf epithelial cells with bacteria present. Patient treated with IV fluids, IV antibiotics, IV PPIs, IV antiemetics and other supportive measures. USG abdomen/pelvis showed Grade-I fatty liver changes and mildly edematous ascending colon up to hepatic flexure (suggestive of colitis), cholelithiasis, minimal ascites, and minimal right pleural effusion with underlying subsegmental lung consolidation. Repeat serum creatinine (1.17mg/dl) improved to normal. TSH and Free T4 were normal. Stool routine showed 2-3/hpf RBCs, plenty/hpf pus cells. Patient was advised to stay for further management but attendants not willing; hence discharged at request.

procedures.value: 1) USG Abdomen and Pelvis (Bedside); 2) Laboratory investigations including CBC, serum creatinine, serum electrolytes, urine routine, stool routine, ABG with lactate, TSH, Free T4; 3) Urine culture and sensitivity (result pending)
